summary: Iteration 2 — verified all 7 requested responsive refinements on the Astro portfolio at /portfolio-website across viewports 360/390/430/768/1440px. 6 of 7 refinements fully pass. Only Refinement 2 partially fails: the scroll-triggered nav opacity boost (`.nav.is-scrolled`) never activates because <body> is the actual scroll container (due to both html+body having overflow-x: hidden) while Nav.astro attaches its scroll listener to `window` — so the listener never fires and content stays readable through the semi-transparent glass while the user scrolls. All other refinements (mobile section spacing, scroll-padding-top / scroll-margin-top, mobile nav height 62px, avatar caption, 2× ticker with correct shift, mobile ticker font 16.8px + 32px gap, square 4/3/2 gallery, lightbox counter 1/6 with object-fit: contain + max-height 84vh, mobile card density 22px padding, no horizontal overflow at any viewport, anchored section offset, theme persistence, Cmd/Ctrl+K → Escape, filter chips, contact mailto note) verified passing.

ui_bugs.issue: The scroll-elevation class `.nav.is-scrolled` is NEVER applied while scrolling, so the nav glass never boosts from ~rgba(13,13,15,0.78) → 0.94. Root cause: global.css sets `overflow-x: hidden` on BOTH html and body, which causes the browser to make <body> the scrolling container (document.body.scrollTop increments, but window.scrollY and document.documentElement.scrollTop stay 0 and no `scroll` event fires on window). The listener in Nav.astro (`window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, {passive:true})`) therefore never triggers on real user scroll. Visible symptom on the home page: while scrolling, hero content / expertise-ticker words remain clearly readable through the floating nav (see screenshot — 'UI/UX', 'Social Media', 'Branding' visible behind the pill nav).

verified_refinements.R1_mobile_section_spacing: PASS — at 360/390/430 all main sections have padding 56px top/bottom, no min-height:100vh/svh on non-hero sections; scrollWidth == innerWidth.
verified_refinements.R2_sticky_nav: PARTIAL — scroll-padding-top:108px on html, scroll-margin-top:98px on section[id]; anchored jump to #capabilities lands with section top 97.7 vs nav bottom 82 (>=nav-bottom, no overlap). Mobile --nav-height stays 62px. BUT nav opacity boost on scroll DOES NOT WORK (see ui_bugs above).
verified_refinements.R3_avatar_caption: PASS — hero caption reads '01 · Shubham Pendse' (left) and 'DESIGNER' (right), sourced from home.json hero.avatar_caption.left/right.
verified_refinements.R4_ticker: PASS — .ticker__item count = 14 (2× duplication of 7 base items), --ticker-shift = calc(-50% - 24px). Mobile @390: ticker item font-size 16.8px (~1.05rem), gap 32px, mobile animation-duration 32s. Mask-image fade edges present (correct per spec).
verified_refinements.R5_gallery_lightbox: PASS — /projects/visol-india gallery uses 4 cols @desktop (276×276 square), 3 cols @768 with 14px gap, 2 cols @390 with 12px gap; aspect-ratio 1/1; object-fit: cover. Hero image no longer has data-lightbox → only 6 lightbox images. Lightbox counter reads '1 / 6'. .lbx__stage img computed max-height 756px (84vh @900px) and object-fit: contain.
verified_refinements.R6_mobile_card_density: PASS — .cap padding at 390px = 22px top / 20px left (was 32px on desktop). Cards still readable.
verified_refinements.R7_responsive_qa: MOSTLY PASS — no horizontal overflow (sw==iw) at 360/390/430/768/1440 on Home and Work. No heading overlap with nav during scroll on Home/Work/About/Contact/Project. Theme toggle persists ('theme' key set in localStorage, matches data-theme). Ctrl+K opens command menu (data-cmd shows), Escape closes it. Filter chips on /work still filter (URL updates to ?category=social-media). Contact form still shows mailto informational note. Only R7 mini-failure: same nav-opacity issue (see R2).

action_items: Fix Nav.astro scroll listener so `.is-scrolled` class is applied when the user scrolls. Root cause: <body> is the scroll container. Simplest fixes (pick one): (a) remove `overflow-x: hidden` from `body` in global.css (keep only on html) so window/documentElement becomes the scroll root — `window.scrollY` will then update and the existing scroll listener will fire, (b) attach the scroll listener to `document.body` (or the actual scrolling element resolved via `document.scrollingElement`) instead of `window`, or (c) read scroll position from `document.scrollingElement.scrollTop || window.scrollY` and listen with `document.addEventListener('scroll', ..., true)` (capture phase catches scroll on body). Verify after fix that .nav__inner bg goes from rgba(13,13,15,0.78) → rgba(10,10,12,0.94) on dark theme and rgba(253,250,244,0.95) on light theme after scrolling >24px.
critical_code_review_comments: global.css sets `overflow-x: hidden` on BOTH `html` and `body`. This is the underlying cause that shifts the scroll container onto <body> and breaks any code that assumes window-level scrolling (Nav elevation here, and potentially any future scroll-linked reveal / progress bar). Consider dropping the body rule and keeping only html.

Nav.astro binds scroll listener to `window` — brittle if scroll root shifts. Recommend using `document.scrollingElement` and listening on `document` with capture=true, or a passive listener on `window` + fallback readout of `document.scrollingElement.scrollTop`.
Consistency: keep the mask-fade edges on the ticker (that's intentional and looks intentional in dark theme) — verified no word is chopped mid-letter within the readable center band.
Lightbox correctly no longer includes cs-hero image; counter now shows 1/6 as spec'd — good fix.
Refinement wins: excellent scroll-margin-top / scroll-padding-top setup — anchored navigation lands cleanly below the pill nav on all pages.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (24 of 25 individual checks pass; single failing check is the nav-scroll-elevation class not firing)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Static Astro site under /portfolio-website. The ONE remaining bug in iteration_2: window scroll events do NOT fire because <body> is the scrolling container (both html+body have overflow-x: hidden). To reproduce: `page.mouse.wheel(0, 800)` — after this, `document.body.scrollTop == 800` but `window.scrollY == 0` and `document.documentElement.scrollTop == 0`, and `.nav.is-scrolled` is not applied. When retesting, verify that (a) after 500px scroll `.nav.is-scrolled` class is present, (b) `getComputedStyle(nav__inner).backgroundColor` differs measurably from the pre-scroll value — expect approx rgba(10,10,12,0.94) on dark, rgba(253,250,244,0.95) on light. Everything else in this iteration passes; do not re-litigate the mask-fade on the ticker (that is intentional).

rca_of_the_issue.bug: Sticky nav opacity boost never activates on scroll — Refinement 2 partially broken.
reproduction_steps: 3. Scroll: page.mouse.wheel(0, 800) — page visibly scrolls, capability cards come into view.
5. .nav.is-scrolled classList absent; nav bg still 'rgba(13, 13, 15, 0.78)'; ticker words visibly readable through nav glass.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: html { overflow-x: hidden } combined with body { overflow-x: hidden } shifts the scrolling container onto <body>. window scroll events do not fire (only body scroll fires). Nav.astro attaches listener to window → never called → .is-scrolled class never added → nav__inner bg never boosted to 0.94.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Choose one: (A) Drop `overflow-x: hidden` from body in /app/src/styles/global.css (keep only on html). (B) In Nav.astro, listen on the true scroll root: `const scroller = document.scrollingElement || document.body; document.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, {capture:true, passive:true}); const onScroll = () => (scroller.scrollTop > 24 ? nav.classList.add('is-scrolled') : nav.classList.remove('is-scrolled'));`. Option A also improves any future scroll-progress bars / reveal-on-view logic that depends on window scroll.
